purpose: Source-type registry for citation-first Islamic learning. This file records candidate providers, storage rules, citation requirements, and risks before UI implementation.
global_rules: Do not store or display copied Quran, hadith, dua, adhkar, Arabic, transliteration, or translation text unless the exact source, license, and attribution requirements are verified.
Do not claim institutional or scholarly endorsement.
Do not present DeenAI as a religious ruling system.
Do not infer hadith grades, Quran interpretation, dua benefits, or legal rulings from model knowledge.
Any generated answer must cite source records retrieved for the current request.

allowed_use: Metadata planning for Quran Arabic lookup.
Future verbatim display only after terms review, checksum tracking, version tracking, and attribution requirements are implemented.
restricted_use: No modified Quran text.
No display without source attribution.
No model-generated Quran Arabic.
No local text storage before checksum and current terms review.
source_categories.notes: Use canonical surah and ayah identifiers. Preserve selected edition exactly if future ingestion is approved.

allowed_use: Metadata planning for Quran lookup, translation lookup, search, and citation-card structure.
Future API-linked retrieval after access and terms review.
restricted_use: No local cache of API content unless terms allow it.
No display of translations without translator or edition attribution.
No republishing of API content beyond provider terms.
source_categories.notes: Useful API surface, but translation, tafsir, audio, and derived content need field-level permission review.

allowed_use: Backup metadata planning for Quran API lookup.
Future API-linked lookup if primary API is unavailable and terms review is complete.
restricted_use: No local content storage before terms review.
No translation display without edition attribution.
No recitation or audio use without separate rights review.
source_categories.notes: Backup role only. Translation and recitation rights may differ by edition.

allowed_use: Metadata planning for hadith lookup and citation-card structure.
Future API-linked retrieval after API access, terms review, and field-level display policy.
restricted_use: No copied bulk data.
No hadith text display before access and permission review.
No generated hadith wording.
No grade display unless grade metadata is directly provided by the retrieved record.
source_categories.notes: Hadith numbering, translation, grading metadata, and collection scope are sensitive. Missing metadata should trigger source-missing or citation-only behavior.

allowed_use: Metadata planning for dua and adhkar lookup.
Future display only after a specific edition, translation, transliteration, and permission status are documented.
restricted_use: No copied dua text before provenance and permission review.
No generated Arabic wording.
No generated transliteration.
No invented usage benefits or outcomes.
source_categories.notes: Dua text and transliteration errors can change meaning. Treat unsourced dua wording as blocked.

allowed_use: Metadata planning for calculated prayer-time responses.
Future API-linked calculation with method, date, location, timezone, and caveat display.
restricted_use: No presentation as a universal local authority.
No omission of calculation method.
No omission of local mosque or institutional variance caveat.
source_categories.notes: Computed prayer times may differ by method and local institution. Output must be framed as calculated provider data.

allowed_use: Internal policy reference for source-missing behavior, refusal boundaries, escalation routing, and citation requirements.
restricted_use: No use as a primary religious source.
No replacement for Quran, hadith, dua, or prayer-time provider records.
source_categories.notes: Internal policy source used by the stub pipeline when a query is blocked before external retrieval.
